summary: Matched and partially decompiled a batch of gmresult result-screen functions, adding a dedicated gmresult.static.h with ResultsData/ResultsPlayerData layout, replacing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures, and propagating related type fixes such as MatchOutcome and GXColor usage. decomp.dev reported 9 new full matches in main/melee/gm/gmresult and 9 additional unmatched-function improvements, for +416 matched bytes and +0.01% overall matched code.
what_changed: Implemented or partially implemented multiple gmresult functions previously left as placeholder comments, including fn_80174284, fn_80174338, fn_8017435C, fn_80174380, gm_801743A4, fn_80174920, fn_801749B8, fn_80174FD0, fn_80175038, fn_8017507C, fn_80175240, fn_8017556C, fn_80175C5C, fn_80175D34, fn_80176A6C, fn_80176BCC, fn_80176D18, and gm_80177704_OnLeave.

Added src/melee/gm/gmresult.static.h containing ResultsPlayerData and ResultsData, including a STATIC_ASSERT that ResultsData is 0x5A8 bytes and static storage for lbl_8046DBE8 plus small static globals lbl_804D3FA0, lbl_804D3FA4, and lbl_804D3FB0.
Moved the old anonymous lbl_8046DBE8_t-style layout out of gmresult.c into a more descriptive ResultsData layout and forward-declared ResultsData in gm/forward.h.
Added MatchOutcome enum values in gm/forward.h and changed gm_801743A4 to accept enum MatchOutcome rather than an untyped u8.

Typed gmresult.h prototypes for newly worked functions, replacing many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete return and argument types such as MatchEnd*, s32, void, HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, GXColor, Vec3*, ResultsData*, and ResultsPlayerData*.
Refined MatchEnd in gm/types.h by replacing a large pad at x44C with an x44C[4] array of UnkResultPlayerData containing a 0x100 byte buffer and padding to 0x508.
Changed gm_80160968 and lbl_803B7864 from U8Vec4 to GXColor, including the public header and static header.
Adjusted grstadium.c color handling to include GXStruct.h, use GXColor locals, access r/g/b fields instead of x/y/z, and cast between U8Vec4 and GXColor where existing data/layout required it.
Removed an include of gm_unsplit.h from gmvsmelee.c.
Added GXStruct.h include to sislib.h, supporting use of GXColor in SisLib-related declarations or callers.
smart_moves: Introduced ResultsData and ResultsPlayerData with offsets and a size assertion before using deeper fields throughout gmresult.c, reducing reliance on raw padding while protecting ABI/layout correctness.

Kept uncertain field purposes documented with cautious comments such as flags, page, winner?, team winner?, main proc?, winner logo?, and stats_text page guesses instead of overnaming beyond evidence.
Converted color-returning helper gm_80160968 to GXColor, which better matches r/g/b/a channel access in result-screen code and made the new gmresult color code more readable.
Used a dedicated MatchOutcome enum and named OUTCOME_LRASTART/OUTCOME_RETRY logic, making gm_801743A4 intent more searchable than numeric outcome checks.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK calls and explicit casts between GXColor/U8Vec4 where needed.
Scoped static result-screen internals into gmresult.static.h, allowing gmresult.c and gmresult.h to use the same struct names without exposing every internal field globally.
decomp_lessons: For result-screen work, investing in a local static header with a size-asserted global state struct can unlock many small matches and partial matches by replacing pad-only globals with typed access.
When a function manipulates RGBA channels, prefer GXColor over generic U8Vec4 if call sites access r/g/b/a semantics; this can improve both readability and prototype accuracy.
Forward-declare new cross-file structs and enums in gm/forward.h when public headers need them, instead of pulling full static layouts everywhere.

Typed helper prototypes in adjacent modules can be necessary for matching newly decompiled callers; this PR updated gm_17AD.h stat-access helpers to s32 fn(s32) because gmresult callers use integer stat values.
Partial decompilation should still update public prototypes to the strongest known types, even when some functions remain unmatched.
Keep unknown fields named conservatively with offsets and optional comments until their semantics are proven.
naming_conventions: Result-screen global state was renamed from a label-derived lbl_8046DBE8_t concept to struct ResultsData while retaining the static global label lbl_8046DBE8.
Per-player result-screen state uses struct ResultsPlayerData and groups stats_text as stats_text[3][10], with comments indicating suspected pages and entries.
Match outcome enum constants use OUTCOME_* names, with known names OUTCOME_NONE, OUTCOME_TIMEOUT, OUTCOME_ELIMINATION, OUTCOME_LRASTART, and OUTCOME_RETRY and unknown values left as OUTCOME_0xN.
Unknown or not-yet-understood ResultsData fields remain offset-style names such as x0, x1, x4, x5, x6, x8, xC, x20, x24, x34, x4C, and x94.
Existing decomp label/function naming conventions are preserved for unknown functions, e.g. fn_80174284 and gm_801743A4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in several functions, including fn_80174284, fn_8017507C, fn_80175240, fn_80175C5C, and fn_80175D34, indicating stack-shaping for matching.
Kept exact-looking small wrapper functions for audio and animation calls, such as lbAudioAx_80024030 wrappers and HSD_JObjAnimAll wrappers, which decomp.dev counted as full matches.
Matched enum range logic in gm_801743A4 with an unsigned cast expression: (u8)(outcome - OUTCOME_LRASTART) <= OUTCOME_TIMEOUT.
Clamped displayed stat values and color channel calculations with explicit float-to-integer assignments, preserving compiler-sensitive behavior.
Used casts between GXColor* and U8Vec4* in grstadium.c to reconcile more semantic color typing with pre-existing U8Vec4 data/layout.
Created typed access to MatchEnd.x44C[slot].x0 as a 0x100 byte buffer for loops counting result data, rather than indexing into a raw padding region.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 9 new full matches: fn_80174FD0, fn_80175038, fn_80174338, fn_8017435C, fn_80174380, fn_80176BCC, fn_80176D18, gm_801743A4, and gm_80177704_OnLeave.
decomp.dev bot reported 9 improved-but-not-fully-matched functions: fn_80175240, fn_8017507C, fn_8017556C, fn_80176A6C, fn_80175C5C, fn_801749B8, fn_80174284, fn_80174920, and fn_80175D34.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ResultsData fields x0/x1/x4/x5/x6/x94 or ResultsPlayerData fields after more gmresult decompilation.
Search for follow-up fixes around GXColor versus U8Vec4 casts in grstadium.c, especially assignments to sp28[0].r/g/b.

Look for later matches of gmresult functions left as placeholders in this PR: fn_801743C4, fn_80174468, fn_801748EC, fn_80174A60, fn_80174B4C, fn_801756E0, fn_80175880, fn_80175A94, fn_80175DC8, fn_80176BF0, fn_80176D3C, fn_80176F60, fn_801771C0, and gm_80177368_OnEnter.
Search for PRs that further type gm_17AD stat helper functions fn_8017AD28, fn_8017AE0C, fn_8017B010, and fn_8017B21C.
Check whether MatchOutcome unknown constants OUTCOME_0x3 through OUTCOME_0x9 receive semantic names later.
